Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge.

 

Overview:

Join our Product Management team and become a driving force behind the Master Data Management Product (MDM) group. In this pivotal role, you'll delve into market trends across our existing and target customer segments, transforming insights into actionable business requirements, functional use cases, and compelling product solutions involving AI. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, you'll emerge as the subject matter expert on MDM, guiding product development and supporting sales with your deep product knowledge and market insights. Your expertise will identify growth opportunities, inform strategy, and ensure our products continue to meet the evolving needs of our market.

What you will do:

  • Manage Product Requirements: Gather and prioritize product needs from customers, sales, support, and other teams. Create clear documentation (MRDs and PRDs), and work with Engineering to build the roadmaps.
  • Develop Business Plans: Collaborate and contribute to create long-term business plans for selected market segments. Track performance and update stakeholders regularly.
  • Build Business Cases: Support product decisions, use of AI, and partnerships with solid business models and justifications.
  • Communicate Effectively: Share updates clearly through documents, emails, and presentations. Ensure stakeholders understand market trends and product direction.
  • Provide Leadership: Be the voice of the customer and influence the direction of the product.
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Marketing, Engineering, Strategic Planning, and other product teams to make informed decisions and expand product influence.
  • Track Success Metrics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators to ensure goals are being met.

What we are looking for:

  • Experience: 8+ years of product management experience building data products, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields is helpful but not required.
  • Product Management Expertise: Demonstrated ability to design and deliver solutions specifically in product information management or other data domains—such as material master, customer master, reference data, etc.
  • Experience with a broad spectrum of data management practices, including data quality, data integration, data governance, and metadata management.
  • Experience with AI: Demonstrated expertise in justifying and embedding AI in products.
  • Hybrid Cloud Expertise: Experience with hybrid cloud deployments and SaaS solutions, particularly in AWS.
  • Strategic & Execution Skills: Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams and a high-volume feature backlog.
  • Conceptual Technical Proficiency: Comfortable partnering with engineering on high-level architectural decisions involving APIs, integrations, platform services, and hybrid deployment models.
  • Tools & Methodology Experience: Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha!. Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and lead cross-functional initiatives across product lines.
  • Enthusiasm to work in a fast-paced, demanding business environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional groups, management, sales and customers.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage high volumes of work
  • Excellent analytical, interpersonal, managerial, and organizational skills

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

descriptionHtml:
Application and Interview Impersonation Notice:  Impersonating another individual when applying for employment, and/or participating in an interview process to assist another individual in obtaining employment, with Precisely Software Incorporated (“Precisely”) is unlawful.  If Precisely identifies such fraudulent conduct, then as applicable and to the extent permitted by law, the application will be rejected, an offer (if made) will be rescinded, or the employment will be terminated, and legal action may be taken against the impersonators.

Precisely is the leader in data integrity. We empower businesses to make more confident decisions based on trusted data through a unique combination of software, data enrichment products and strategic services. What does this mean to you? For starters, it means joining a company focused on delivering outstanding innovation and support that helps customers increase revenue, lower costs and reduce risk. In fact, Precisely powers better decisions for more than 12,000 global organizations, including 95 of the Fortune 100. Precisely's 2500 employees are unified by four company core values that are central to who we are and how we operate: Openness, Determination, Individuality, and Collaboration. We are committed to career development for our employees and offer opportunities for growth, learning and building community. With a "work from anywhere" culture, we celebrate diversity in a distributed environment with a presence in 30 countries as well as 20 offices in over 5 continents. Learn more about why it's an exciting time to join Precisely!

Precisely is an AI-first organization. All employees are expected to demonstrate proficiency in applying AI tools to accelerate their work, improve output quality, and eliminate low-value tasks. Candidates should be comfortable using generative AI tools (e.g., Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT) in their day-to-day workflows, able to evaluate AI-generated outputs critically, and open to continuously adopting new AI capabilities as they emerge.

 

Overview:

Join our Product Management team and become a driving force behind the Master Data Management Product (MDM) group. In this pivotal role, you'll delve into market trends across our existing and target customer segments, transforming insights into actionable business requirements, functional use cases, and compelling product solutions involving AI. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, you'll emerge as the subject matter expert on MDM, guiding product development and supporting sales with your deep product knowledge and market insights. Your expertise will identify growth opportunities, inform strategy, and ensure our products continue to meet the evolving needs of our market.

What you will do:

  • Manage Product Requirements: Gather and prioritize product needs from customers, sales, support, and other teams. Create clear documentation (MRDs and PRDs), and work with Engineering to build the roadmaps.
  • Develop Business Plans: Collaborate and contribute to create long-term business plans for selected market segments. Track performance and update stakeholders regularly.
  • Build Business Cases: Support product decisions, use of AI, and partnerships with solid business models and justifications.
  • Communicate Effectively: Share updates clearly through documents, emails, and presentations. Ensure stakeholders understand market trends and product direction.
  • Provide Leadership: Be the voice of the customer and influence the direction of the product.
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Marketing, Engineering, Strategic Planning, and other product teams to make informed decisions and expand product influence.
  • Track Success Metrics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators to ensure goals are being met.

What we are looking for:

  • Experience: 8+ years of product management experience building data products, shared services, or internal-facing capabilities used by multiple product teams.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. Background in Computer Science, Information Systems, Engineering, or related fields is helpful but not required.
  • Product Management Expertise: Demonstrated ability to design and deliver solutions specifically in product information management or other data domains—such as material master, customer master, reference data, etc.
  • Experience with a broad spectrum of data management practices, including data quality, data integration, data governance, and metadata management.
  • Experience with AI: Demonstrated expertise in justifying and embedding AI in products.
  • Hybrid Cloud Expertise: Experience with hybrid cloud deployments and SaaS solutions, particularly in AWS.
  • Strategic & Execution Skills: Proven ability to define platform standards, manage complex stakeholder groups, and drive execution in an Agile environment with multiple dependent teams and a high-volume feature backlog.
  • Conceptual Technical Proficiency: Comfortable partnering with engineering on high-level architectural decisions involving APIs, integrations, platform services, and hybrid deployment models.
  • Tools & Methodology Experience: Hands-on experience with Agile development practices and product tools such as Aha!. Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and lead cross-functional initiatives across product lines.
  • Enthusiasm to work in a fast-paced, demanding business environment while collaborating effectively with cross-functional groups, management, sales and customers.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage high volumes of work
  • Excellent analytical, interpersonal, managerial, and organizational skills

The personal data that you provide as a part of this job application will be handled in accordance with relevant laws. For more information about how Precisely handles the personal data of job applicants, please see the Precisely Candidate Privacy Notice

title: Senior Manager SEO – SaaS Product SEO / AEO ( 7+ years )

consistent product terminology across the siteImplement/validate structured data and on-page modules that increase extraction (FAQs, steps, specs, pricing/plan tables where applicable)Measure AI visibility via tracked prompts/queries, citation mentions, and overlap with priority clusters—then iterate content and schemaInterview expectation: bring one example of an AEO/SEO experiment or case study (problem → approach → results → learnings)Brand & product launch:Partner with Product Marketing on new product launches: positioning, naming/terminology, launch pages, and message consistency across web propertiesBuild launch-ready content systems (landing page templates, comparison pages, FAQs, proof blocks) that support both brand trust and conversionNice-to-have: experience running or collaborating on paid acquisition (search/social) and using insights to improve organic + AI visibilityLeadership & operating model:Lead analysts and content partners with clear priorities, QA standards, and weekly reportingRun a monthly roadmap across content, technical SEO, and AEO/GEO initiatives with measurable outcomesCoach execution and ensure repeatable systems (briefs, templates, internal linking rules, dashboards)Responsibilities:Own the SEO + AEO/GEO roadmap for SaaS acquisition across Google and AI answer enginesImprove performance of existing ranking pages (top 10 → top 3 where strategic) and expand clusters to grow qualified pipelinePartner with engineering on technical SEO (crawl/index, templates, CWV, JS rendering, structured data)Build topical authority via digital PR/backlinks and partner content where it supports product growthSupport new product launches with SEO/AEO-ready messaging and launch pages; align with Product Marketing on brand narrative and terminologySet measurement: dashboards for rankings, non-brand traffic, conversions, and AI citations; communicate insights to stakeholdersManage team execution, quality, and delivery cadence in an AI-first search environmentQualifications:7+ years in SEO for SaaS or product-led businesses; experience owning outcomes (traffic, pipeline, signups)Demonstrated wins improving already-ranking pages and expanding keyword clusters with clear measurementHands-on with Google Search Console, GA4, and at least one major SEO suite (Ahrefs/SEMrush) plus crawler tools (e.g., Screaming Frog)Strong technical SEO fundamentals and ability to partner with developers to ship fixesPractical exposure to AEO/GEO with a repeatable approach to improving AI citations/mentionsNice-to-have experience with paid search / performance marketing collaboration (ads copy, landing page testing, incrementality) to accelerate launchesPeople leadership: hiring/coaching, prioritization, and stakeholder communication
descriptionHtml:
Senior Manager SEO – SaaS Product SEO / AEO ( 7+ years )

Experience: 7 + years

Location: Remote (Work from Home)

Employment Type: Full-time

No of Positions: 2–3

Notice Period: Immediate Joiners Preferred

Industry: SaaS / AI Platforms / IT Services

Educational Qualification: Bachelor degree in Engineering from Tier I/Master’s degree from Tier I


Role Overview: Lead product SEO and AEO/GEO for a SaaS platform, partnering with Product Marketing on new product launches and brand messaging, and driving measurable growth across Google SERPs and AI answer engines through scalable content, technical SEO, and entity-first optimization.


Success Metrics (example targets): Define and report weekly on non-brand organic signups/MQLs, top-3 ranking gains, organic share-of-voice in priority clusters (including launch pages), AI-citation wins (AI Overviews/ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity), and brand lift signals such as branded search growth and direct traffic contribution.


Role Summary & Requirements:

You will own SEO growth for a SaaS product—improving existing rankings, expanding high-intent keyword coverage, and increasing visibility in AI answer engines via structured content, entities, and technical excellence.

Must-have experience:

  • Scaled already-ranking pages (e.g., top 10 → top 3) through on-page, internal linking, and intent/CTR improvements
  • Built and executed keyword cluster expansion around existing winners to grow qualified organic pipeline
  • Delivered measurable visibility in AI answer engines (AI Overviews/ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity) using structured data + entity optimization
  • Operated in a product + engineering environment (shipping technical SEO fixes, templates, and scalable content systems)

Core competencies:

  • Intent mapping across SaaS funnels (product-led, solutions, comparisons) with clear page-to-keyword ownership
  • Internal linking and information architecture to strengthen topical clusters
  • Content briefs, editorial QA, and experimentation (titles/FAQs/modules) to lift rankings and conversions

Technical SEO (hands-on or strongly partnered with engineering):

  • Crawl/index control, templates, and site architecture improvements that move rankings
  • JavaScript rendering and performance (Core Web Vitals) diagnostics and fixes with dev teams
  • Schema/structured data strategy (e.g., Product, FAQ, HowTo, Breadcrumb, Organization) to improve SERP + AI readability

AEO / GEO (AI Search Optimization):

  • Create citation-ready pages: clear entities, definitions, comparisons, and evidence blocks; consistent product terminology across the site
  • Implement/validate structured data and on-page modules that increase extraction (FAQs, steps, specs, pricing/plan tables where applicable)
  • Measure AI visibility via tracked prompts/queries, citation mentions, and overlap with priority clusters—then iterate content and schema
  • Interview expectation: bring one example of an AEO/SEO experiment or case study (problem → approach → results → learnings)

Brand & product launch:

  • Partner with Product Marketing on new product launches: positioning, naming/terminology, launch pages, and message consistency across web properties
  • Build launch-ready content systems (landing page templates, comparison pages, FAQs, proof blocks) that support both brand trust and conversion
  • Nice-to-have: experience running or collaborating on paid acquisition (search/social) and using insights to improve organic + AI visibility

Leadership & operating model:

  • Lead analysts and content partners with clear priorities, QA standards, and weekly reporting
  • Run a monthly roadmap across content, technical SEO, and AEO/GEO initiatives with measurable outcomes
  • Coach execution and ensure repeatable systems (briefs, templates, internal linking rules, dashboards)


Responsibilities:

  • Own the SEO + AEO/GEO roadmap for SaaS acquisition across Google and AI answer engines
  • Improve performance of existing ranking pages (top 10 → top 3 where strategic) and expand clusters to grow qualified pipeline
  • Partner with engineering on technical SEO (crawl/index, templates, CWV, JS rendering, structured data)
  • Build topical authority via digital PR/backlinks and partner content where it supports product growth
  • Support new product launches with SEO/AEO-ready messaging and launch pages; align with Product Marketing on brand narrative and terminology
  • Set measurement: dashboards for rankings, non-brand traffic, conversions, and AI citations; communicate insights to stakeholders
  • Manage team execution, quality, and delivery cadence in an AI-first search environment

Qualifications:

  • 7+ years in SEO for SaaS or product-led businesses; experience owning outcomes (traffic, pipeline, signups)
  • Demonstrated wins improving already-ranking pages and expanding keyword clusters with clear measurement
  • Hands-on with Google Search Console, GA4, and at least one major SEO suite (Ahrefs/SEMrush) plus crawler tools (e.g., Screaming Frog)
  • Strong technical SEO fundamentals and ability to partner with developers to ship fixes
  • Practical exposure to AEO/GEO with a repeatable approach to improving AI citations/mentions
  • Nice-to-have experience with paid search / performance marketing collaboration (ads copy, landing page testing, incrementality) to accelerate launches
  • People leadership: hiring/coaching, prioritization, and stakeholder communication

descriptionHtml:
About Arcserve


Arcserve provides exceptional solutions to protect the priceless digital assets of organizations in need of full scale, comprehensive data protection. Established in 1983, Arcserve is the world’s most experienced provider of business continuity solutions that safeguard every application and system, on every premises and every cloud. Organizations in over 150 countries around the world rely on Arcserve’s highly efficient, integrated technologies and expertise to eliminate the risk of data loss and extended downtime while reducing the cost and complexity of backing up and restoring data by up to 50 percent


Position Overview

Arcserve is seeking an experienced Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy and delivery of data protection and cyber resilience solutions designed for the Managed Service Provider (MSP) market.

This role requires a strong blend of domain expertise in backup, disaster recovery, and cyber resilience, deep understanding of MSP business models, and hands-on experience leveraging AI, automation, and emerging agentic systems to drive innovation.

This is a remote role open to candidates in India.


Key Responsibilities

  • Define and execute product strategy and roadmap for MSP-focused data protection solutions
  • Design capabilities that support multi-tenancy, automation, scalability, and efficient MSP operations
  • Drive innovation in backup, ransomware protection, anomaly detection, and disaster recovery orchestration
  • Identify and deliver AI/automation opportunities, including predictive insights, workflow automation, and agent-driven operations
  • Partner with engineering and data teams to develop intelligent, automation-first features
  • Leverage AI tools to accelerate product workflows (research, documentation, analysis, prototyping)
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams and engage directly with customers and MSP partners
  • Own end-to-end product lifecycle, including metrics, prioritization, and Agile execution


Required Qualifications

  • 7+ years of product management experience in enterprise or B2B SaaS
  • Strong expertise in data protection, backup & recovery, DR, or cyber resilience
  • Proven experience building or managing products for the MSP market
  • Experience using AI tools to enhance PM productivity and workflows
  • Experience defining or contributing to AI/ML, automation, or agent-driven features
  • Solid understanding of MSP operations (multi-tenancy, service delivery, SLAs)
  • Experience with hybrid/cloud environments (AWS, Azure, on-prem)
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills


Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with DRaaS/BaaS or SaaS data protection platforms
  • Familiarity with ransomware detection and cyber recovery workflows
  • Background in infrastructure (virtualization, storage, networking)
  • Experience with subscription or usage-based pricing models

title: Senior Product Manager
description: About Team:US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.Flexible, hybrid workWalmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.BenefitsBeyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.BelongingWe aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.Equal Opportunity EmployerWalmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or related area and 4 years’ experience as a product manager, ideally working on consumer-facing, large-scale, highly complex B2B/C productsMasters: Business AdministrationPrimary Location...Block- 1, Prestige Tech Pacific Park, Sy No. 38/1, Outer Ring Rd Kadubeesanahalli , India
descriptionHtml:
About Team:

US Omni Tech at Walmart Global Tech, Bengaluru, is focused on improving Walmart customer experience across both physical & digital and making Walmart the most exciting place to shop. With 4000+ brick and mortar stores and thousands of other suppliers fulfilling Walmart's online/in-store orders for over 1 billion customers - the challenge and complexity is pronounced! We work within the world’s largest retailer yet maintain a startup feel working in small agile teams to revolutionize the fulfilment landscape. As a leader in the supply chain group, you are accountable and responsible for number of critical products/platforms that help serve our customers in the most cost-effective manner in multiple geographies and businesses.

What you'll do:

Assist in defining product features and translating business needs into user stories and technical requirements.

Partner with engineering and QA teams to plan sprints and ensure timely, high-quality delivery.

Analyze data and feedback to prioritize feature enhancements and resolve issues.

Work with internal stakeholders (e.g., logistics, inventory, store operations) to gather and clarify requirements.

Support in developing product documentation, training materials, and internal communication plans.

Drive product capabilities and roadmap with very minimal supervision and guidance

Be technically savvy to build and grow technical platforms and products as well as contribute to engineering conversations & priorities in a scrappy, ambiguous start-up environment

Be an advocate of metric/data-based decision making, Make impact on a global scale.

Promotes and supports mission, values, and standards of ethics and integrity.

What you'll bring:

You come with 3+ years of product management experience.

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in a cross-functional team

Experience with eCommerce or internet-based products is a plus.

Excellent communication and stakeholder management.

Uncompromising commitment to the customer.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills with ability to champion product solutions throughout the organization.

Advanced/ master’s degree in engineering or management is preferred.

About Walmart Global Tech

Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.

We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Flexible, hybrid work

Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.

Benefits

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.

Belonging

We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Walmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.

Minimum Qualifications...Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.

Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related area and 5 years’ experience in product management. Option 2: 7 years’ experience in product management or related area.

Preferred Qualifications...Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or related area and 4 years’ experience as a product manager, ideally working on consumer-facing, large-scale, highly complex B2B/C products

Masters: Business Administration

Primary Location...Block- 1, Prestige Tech Pacific Park, Sy No. 38/1, Outer Ring Rd Kadubeesanahalli , India

descriptionHtml:
About Us

Our leading SaaS-based Global Employment Platform™ enables clients to expand into over 180 countries quickly and efficiently, without the complexities of establishing local entities. At G-P, we’re dedicated to breaking down barriers to global business and creating opportunities for everyone, everywhere.

Our diverse, remote-first teams are essential to our success. We empower our Dream Team members with flexibility and resources, fostering an environment where innovation thrives and every contribution is valued and celebrated.

The work you do here will positively impact lives around the world. We stand by our promise: Opportunity Made Possible. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, we invite you to join us in expanding your skills and helping to reshape the future of work.

At G-P, we assist organizations in building exceptional global teams in days, not months—streamlining the hiring, onboarding, and management process to unlock growth potential for all.

G-P helps organizations build global teams in minutes, not months. As part of this mission, we’ve created an indispensable AI agent for HR leaders, G-P Gia™ .

Gia is our AI-powered global HR agent that provides HR compliance guidance instantly. Built on over a decade of global employment and legal expertise and 100,000+ vetted articles, Gia analyzes and generates compliant documents and delivers the answers that HR leaders trust — reducing reliance on outside legal counsel and cutting compliance costs by up to 95%.

About The Position

We’re building GIA - the AI for General Counsel, in-house legal teams, and HR teams — helping them operate faster and smarter before problems escalate. GIA is structured like a Startup that demands extreme ownership and relentless speed and a bias for shipping over talking. If you need permission to act, this isn't for you.

This is a co-founder-level PM role reporting into the Head of Product and Engineering: you own the product end-to-end, talk to customers daily, design solutions, write specs, and ship. No swim lanes, no waiting for permission.

What You Will Do

  • Extreme Product Ownership — discovery, design, specs, delivery, outcomes
  • Build the product-led sales engine — onboarding, activation, upgrade triggers, PQLs, viral loops
  • Drive integrations — CLMs, HRIS, doc management, email; define data models and API contracts with particular focus on permission models and ethical walls.
  • Run product research — competitive analysis, user interviews, workflow mapping, market sizing; synthesize into clear product direction
  • Talk to GCs, legal ops, and HR leaders constantly — build conviction from real workflows, not feature requests
  • Ship fast — cut scope, measure what matters, kill what doesn’t
  • Work across time zones — distributed team across US, India, Ireland, Poland

Minimum Requirements

What we're looking for:

  • Engineer background — you can read code and reason about systems
  • Strong product sense and design ability — problem to wireframe to shipped product
  • Product research chops — you run discovery, synthesize qualitative and quantitative inputs, and turn research into roadmap decisions
  • Agentic AI fluency — you use Cursor agents, Claude, and similar tools daily
  • Product-led sales experience — you’ve built self-serve funnels, activation flows, and usage-based sales signals
  • Extreme ownership and speed — you default to action and own outcomes
  • Relentless curiosity — contract law, Kubernetes, behavioral psychology, whatever it takes
  • Design taste and chops — You can wireframe and develop mid fidelity prototypes where required without a designer
  • Core Data Analysis Skills — you can work with product usage data, compliance datasets, and qualitative research to generate insights.
  • Process experience - you can translate real-world HR and legal processes into the product by encoding workflows and business logic

Preferred Qualifications

  • Past founder or co-founder
  • Legal or HR domain experience, particularly GC or legal ops
  • Integration experience — PRDs covering auth, data mapping, error handling, sync strategies

How We Evaluate

  • Critique our product live — directness and taste over diplomacy
  • Solve a real problem we’re facing — on the spot
  • Whiteboard an integration architecture
  • Show us something you’ve built that you’re proud of

Actual compensation for this position may vary and will depend on multiple factors such as relevant qualifications, experience, education, and geographic location. For Full-Time Regular Employees, this position is also eligible for additional compensation as follows:

  • Sales Roles: This position is eligible for a commission structure in addition to base salary.
  • Non-Sales Roles: This position is eligible for an annual bonus which is paid dependent on various factors, including and without limitation, individual and company performance in addition to base salary.

Benefits

G-P values its employees and offers excellent benefits and perks including generous paid parental leave, flexible time off, spending accounts, medical insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, sabbatical after 5 years and more.

Individuals residing, or applying to work, in the United States: California or Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, please review the following additional information:

G-P will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in accordance with the California Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles City Fair Chance Act Ordinance, Los Angeles County Fair Chance Act Ordinance, and San Francisco Fair Chance Act Ordinance. Los Angeles applicants can review additional information regarding the Los Angeles City Fair Chance Act here: Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and Philadelphia applicants can review information pertaining to Philadelphia’s Fair Criminal Record Screening Standards Ordinance here: Fair Chance Poster. Any consideration of a candidate’s background check with arrest or conviction records will include an individualized assessment based on the factors required by applicable law, including the candidate’s specific record and the duties and requirements of the specific job.

G-P. Global Made Possible.

G-P is a proud Equal Opportunity Employer, and we are committed to building and maintaining a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture that celebrates authenticity. We prohibit discrimination and harassment against employees or applicants on the basis of race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, and pregnancy-related conditions), gender identity or expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, physical or mental disability, genetic information, or any other legally protected status.

G-P also is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. Individuals with disabilities are encouraged to apply for these positions. If you need an accommodation due to a disability during the interview process, please contact us at careers@g-p.com.

descriptionHtml:
HackerRank helps companies like NVIDIA, Amazon, and Microsoft hire and upskill the next generation of developers based on skills, not pedigree. Our platform is trusted by over 2,500 of the world’s most innovative companies to build strong engineering teams ready for what’s next.

Software has entered an era where humans and AI build side by side. As this shift accelerates, the definition of strong technical talent is changing. We give companies better ways to identify and invest in next-generation skills.

People at HackerRank care deeply about the impact of their work and sweat the small details so our customers can be wildly successful with products they genuinely love to use. We move with urgency and believe great outcomes come from high standards

About The Role

We're looking for a Product Manager to drive the product strategy for Screen, HackerRank's core hiring platform. This is a high-impact role where you'll shape how companies evaluate technical talent at scale, working at the cutting edge of AI-powered assessment.

You'll own the future of candidate evaluation—defining how we leverage AI to assess technical skills, problem-solving ability, and collaboration in ways that are both innovative and trustworthy. This is cutting-edge AI evaluation work that's defining new standards for the industry.

The platform operates at significant scale and carries a meaningful technical legacy, serving customers of various sizes, shapes, and needs—from startups running a handful of interviews to enterprises processing thousands of assessments. This isn't about greenfield product work; it's about making sophisticated decisions in a constrained environment and delivering measurable impact.

The role comes with high visibility across the organization, including direct exposure to our CEO, and requires close collaboration with platform teams working on integrity, IDE infrastructure, and AI capabilities.

What You'll Do

  • Own the product strategy and roadmap for Screen, making critical decisions about how we evolve our core evaluation platform while balancing innovation with platform stability
  • Drive AI-first evaluation capabilities, defining what signals matter, how AI can surface insights that human reviewers miss, and how to make evaluation both more effective and more fair
  • Navigate technical complexity at scale, working deeply with a team of X engineers (including X staff engineers) and an Engineering Manager to balance technical constraints with user needs
  • Balance competing needs across multiple personas—recruiters, hiring managers, and candidates—designing solutions that work across their often conflicting requirements
  • Guide customers through platform evolution, spending time understanding their hiring challenges, managing migrations to new experiences, and ensuring we solve real problems
  • Collaborate across teams and time zones, working with multiple product and platform teams, and often serving as the bridge between technical complexity and customer value
  • Lead with visibility and impact, working directly with senior leadership and driving cross-functional initiatives that shape the future of technical hiring

Who You Are

  • You've built B2B SaaS products at scale where you balanced competing needs across different user personas and made tradeoffs that worked for multiple stakeholders
  • You're comfortable with complex, legacy systems and don't need every problem to be new or sexy—you can get excited about intricate technical challenges and the hard work of improving something that already exists at scale
  • You understand the complexity of serving diverse customers—from small teams to large enterprises—and can design solutions that flex across this diversity rather than forcing one-size-fits-all approaches
  • You have strong judgment under pressure and can go deep technically when needed, build empathy across different personas, and synthesize complex constraints into clear decisions
  • You care deeply about customers and see direct customer engagement not as a chore but as essential input for building the right thing

Even better if you have

  • Experience working with legacy systems and driving incremental transformation in constrained environments
  • Background in assessment, evaluation, or EdTech products
  • Experience with AI/ML product development or integrating AI capabilities into existing platforms
  • Track record of managing complex platform migrations while maintaining customer satisfaction

You wil thrive in this role if you

  • You're energized by complexity, not overwhelmed by it. The challenge of navigating a legacy platform serving diverse customers excites you rather than frustrates you.
  • You're comfortable working globally. This role requires collaboration across multiple time zones, including occasional meetings outside standard hours, and you see this as an opportunity rather than a burden.
  • You want high visibility and impact. You're ready to work with senior leadership, including our CEO, and drive decisions that shape how companies around the world hire technical talent.
  • You're passionate about the cutting edge of AI evaluation work. You want to be at the forefront of defining how AI changes candidate assessment and you're excited to push boundaries while maintaining trust and fairness.
  • You balance deep technical engagement with customer empathy. You can dive into technical constraints with staff engineers one hour and guide a customer through a migration the next, finding energy in both.

Want to learn more about HackerRank? Check out HackerRank.com to explore our products, solutions and resources, and dive into our story and mission here.

HackerRank is a proud equal employment opportunity and affirmative action employer. We provide equal opportunity to everyone for employment based on individual performance and qualification. We never discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital, veteran, or disability status. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Linkedin | X | Blog | Instagram | Life@HackerRank

Notice To Prospective HackerRank Job Applicants

  • Our Recruiters use @hackerrank.com email addresses.
  • We never ask for payment or credit check information to apply, interview, or work here.

descriptionHtml:
Congratulations, you have taken the first step towards bagging a career-defining role. Join the team of superheroes that safeguard data wherever it goes.



What should you know about us?


Seclore protects and controls digital assets to help enterprises prevent data theft and achieve compliance. Permissions and access to digital assets can be granularly assigned and revoked, or dynamically set at the enterprise-level, including when shared with external parties. Asset discovery and automated policy enforcement allow enterprises to adapt to changing security threats and regulatory requirements in real-time and at scale.


Know more about us at www.seclore.com



You would love our tribe:


If you are a risk-taker, innovator, and fearless problem solver who loves solving challenges of data security, then

this is the place for you!



A sneak peek into the role:


We are fast growing product company and bringing next generation products in ‘secure your data’ domain. We are one of the few product companies in globe that can protect and control data wherever it travels. This is the opportunity to build an exciting and high impact product. Security is core to what we build, and we’re looking for a Product Manager who’s passionate about building secure, scalable, and user-centric products.


Seclore seeks an experienced Product Manager with deep expertise in managing large-scale data, its architecture, and transforming it into actionable insights, along with strong technical expertise and client-facing skills.


This role involves launching a new product, connecting clients, industry experts, and our technical team to define data-driven use cases and outcomes. Strong communication skills, solid understanding of data platforms and analytics, and efficient project management in complex, data-rich environments are essential.



Here's what you will get to explore:


Requirements Gathering & Definition

  • Collaborate with security architects, engineers, and compliance teams to gather technical, data, and regulatory requirements, including how large-scale data is captured, stored, and protected.
  • Translate complex cybersecurity and data architecture needs into clear product specifications, data contracts, and user stories that guide implementation.
  • Understand end-user journeys and instrument product experiences to gather usage, performance, and security metrics, enabling evaluation of new features’ effectiveness and data-driven decision-making.


Be the Functional Expert

  • Understand the full Seclore product suite functionally and be the expert for the product modules owned by you, including how they integrate with enterprise data platforms and pipelines.
  • Know various user personas, their business context, and their data needs, such as reporting, analytics, and insight generation.
  • Understand the technology environment, including devices, operating systems, browsers, and the underlying data architecture (data flows, storage, APIs, and integrations) that powers product capabilities.


Product Development Support

  • Partner with engineering teams throughout the development lifecycle to ensure secure design and implementation of data flows and storage patterns at scale.
  • Participate in sprint planning, backlog grooming, and daily stand-ups to provide product and data-related guidance, clarify instrumentation requirements, and prioritize insight-generating features.


Security Integration

  • Ensure security is embedded in all stages of the product lifecycle while balancing observability, logging, and analytics needs for large volumes of product data.
  • Work on integrating security tools and frameworks (e.g., SIEM, IAM, DLP,into the product ecosystem and associated data pipelines, ensuring events are captured and modeled for actionable insights.


Technical Documentation

  • Create and maintain detailed documentation for security features, APIs, workflows, data models, and compliance mappings, including how product data is collected and used for analytics.


Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Act as a liaison between product, engineering, security, data, and compliance teams to align on data strategy, metrics, and architecture decisions.
  • Support Product leaders and GTM leaders with technical and data insights, feasibility assessments, and recommendations on how to leverage Seclore data for customer outcomes.


Risk & Compliance Alignment

  • Ensure product features and data handling practices align with internal security policies and external regulations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS), including data retention and access controls.
  • Assist in security audits and risk assessments by providing clear visibility into product data flows, architecture, and evidence from product usage analytics.



We can see the next Entrepreneur at Seclore if you have:


  • A technical degree (Engineering, MCA) and business degree (MBA) from a reputed institute.
  • 5+ years of experience in B2B product management, technical program management, or cybersecurity engineering.
  • Experience working in Agile environments and collaborating closely with engineering teams.
  • Ability to break down complex technical problems into simple, actionable steps.
  • Strong analytical, communication, and organizational skills.
  • Experience working in a software product organization as a product consultant, product owner, business analyst or functional expert in the product team.
  • Proven experience working with large-scale data platforms, understanding data architecture, and using product data to derive actionable insights and drive decision-making.
  • Technical background in software either from software development or systems perspective.


GOOD TO HAVE:


  • Awareness and exposure to User Experience design practices.
  • Experience in information security procedures and practices.
  • Initiative to take up responsibility and ownership of tasks and ensure end to end delivery and completion with the highest level of quality.



Why do we call Seclorites Entrepreneurs not Employees?


  • We value and support those who take the initiative and calculate risks.
  • We have an attitude of a problem solver and an aptitude that is tech agnostic. You get to work with the smartest minds in the business.
  • We are thriving not living. At Seclore, it is not just about work but about creating outstanding employee experiences. Our supportive and open culture enables our team to thrive.



Excited to be the next Entrepreneur, apply today!


Don’t have some of the above points in your resume at the moment? Don’t worry. We will help you build it.


Let’s build the future of data security at Seclore together.
