descriptionHtml:
Organizations everywhere struggle under the crushing costs and complexities of “solutions” that promise to simplify their lives. To create a better experience for their customers and employees. To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. Propel or throttle growth. Business software has become a blocker instead of ways to get work done.



There’s another option. Freshworks With a fresh vision for how the world works.



At Freshworks, we build uncomplicated service software that delivers exceptional customer and employee experiences. Our enterprise-grade solutions are powerful, yet easy to use, and quick to deliver results. Our people-first approach to AI eliminates friction, making employees more effective and organizations more productive. Over 72,000 companies, including Bridgestone, New Balance, Nucor, S&P Global, and Sony Music, trust Freshworks customer experience (CX) and employee experience (EX) software to fuel customer loyalty and service efficiency. And, over 4,500 Freshworks employees make this possible, all around the world.



Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.



Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Manager – Product Analytics (Individual Contributor) to serve as the hands-on Product Analytics Lead for one of our Product Verticals, owning product analytics end-to-end and shaping product strategy through data. This is a senior IC role for an analytics leader who thrives in ambiguity, partners deeply with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Design, and influences product roadmap decisions with strong analytical rigor. You will work with complex product telemetry, customer behavior, and business data to generate actionable insights that improve customer value and business outcomes, in close collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams. You will also mentor 2–4 product analysts, acting as a subject-matter expert and raising the bar for analytics quality, experimentation, and storytelling across the organization.

The Impact You Will Create

  • Shape Product Strategy: You will own end-to-end product analytics for a dedicated vertical, transforming complex product telemetry and customer behavior data into actionable insights that directly dictate the product roadmap.

  • Define the North Star: You will establish, evolve, and own the core metrics and KPIs that align Product, Engineering, Marketing, and GTM teams around customer value and business outcomes.

  • Champion an Experimentation Culture: You will lead rigorous A/B testing initiatives from hypothesis to executive interpretation, embedding a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across all product teams.

  • Elevate the Analytics Standard: Acting as a subject-matter expert, you will mentor 2-4 product analysts and raise the organizational bar for data integrity, advanced statistical analysis, and compelling executive storytelling.

Responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end product analytics for a Product Vertical with high autonomy and ownership
  • Partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, and Design leaders to influence product strategy and roadmap decisions
  • Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams to support growth, adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives through data
  • Translate ambiguous business and product questions across teams into structured analytical problem statements
  • Define, evolve, and own north-star metrics and product KPIs aligned to customer and business outcomes
  • Act as the product analytics owner for instrumentation and telemetry, driving event design, coverage, and data quality
  • Work with Engineering, TPMs, and Data Engineering to ensure scalable, reliable, and trusted data foundations
  • Lead A/B testing and experimentation initiatives from hypothesis to analysis and interpretation
  • Apply statistical techniques (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) to inform decisions
  • Perform deep-dive analyses on large, complex datasets to uncover root causes and high-impact opportunities
  • Build and maintain analytics products such as dashboards, frameworks, and self-serve insights for product and business stakeholders
  • Communicate insights through clear, compelling narratives and executive-ready presentations to senior leadership
  • Mentor 2–4 junior product analysts, providing guidance on analysis design, experimentation, and storytelling
  • Champion best practices in analytics, including data integrity, validation, documentation, and reproducibility
  • Foster a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across product teams

Qualifications

Qualifications & Skills

Core Experience

  • 10+ years of total experience in analytics or data science, with 5–7+ years of hands-on product analytics experience.

  • Deep domain expertise in B2B SaaS environments is highly preferred.

Technical & Data Stack

  • Data Querying & Scripting: Expert proficiency in SQL for managing large, complex datasets, paired with strong hands-on Python skills for exploratory, statistical, and predictive analysis.

  • Data Visualization: Proven experience building and maintaining self-serve analytics products and dashboards using Power BI (or similar tools).

  • AI/GenAI Leverage: Exposure to utilizing AI and GenAI tools to enhance and accelerate analytics workflows.

Analytical Methodologies & Product Thinking

  • Advanced Experimentation: Deep understanding of statistical methods (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) and A/B testing design.

  • SaaS Product Metrics: Expertise in product telemetry, event instrumentation design, funnel analysis, and cohort-based lifecycle analysis.

  • Product Sense: Strong business orientation with the ability to connect raw data insights directly to customer adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives.

Leadership & Communication

  • Executive Storytelling: Excellent communication skills with a proven track record of translating ambiguous data into clear narratives and executive-ready presentations.

  • Strategic Influence: Proven ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments and influence senior product, engineering, and business leadership.

  • Mentorship: Experience guiding and mentoring junior analysts in analysis design, data validation, and storytelling best practices.



Additional Information

At Freshworks, we have fostered an environment that enables everyone to find their true potential, purpose, and passion, welcoming colleagues of all backgrounds, genders, sexual orientations, religions, and ethnicities. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and believe that diversity in the workplace creates a more vibrant, richer environment that boosts the goals of our employees, communities, and business. Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.

descriptionHtml:
Senior Product Manager - Unified Telephony | 100% Remote WFH (India)


About the role: We are seeking a Senior Product Manager - Unified Telephony to lead the evolution of our Office Phone + Contact Center (CCaaS) platform into a single, cloud-native communication system. This role is critical in building a next-generation Unified Telephony Platform that seamlessly connects Contact Center (CCaaS) and Office Phone systems into a single, intelligent, AI-powered communications layer.


Role & Responsibilities:


  • Product Strategy & Roadmap: Define and execute the vision for unified telephony across Office Phone and CCaaS, aligned with business goals and customer needs
  • Telephony Platform Ownership: Lead development of core capabilities, including SIP-based calling, IP PBX, extension dialing, call transfers (warm/cold), voicemail, and presence
  • Architecture & Execution: Partner with engineering on Telephony architecture, call routing, carrier integrations, and scalability
  • Unified Routing Experience: Enable seamless call flows between contact center queues and direct office users (extension-based dialing)
  • Customer & Market Insights: Work closely with customers, sales, and support to identify gaps and drive product improvements
  • Go-to-Market: Support positioning, packaging, and pricing against UCaaS/CCaaS competitors
  • Uptime Focus: Define KPIs (call quality, latency, uptime) and continuously optimize product performance


Qualifications


  • Experience: 6+ years of product management experience, with at least 5+ years focusing on UCaaS and CCaaS solutions in B2B SaaS or financial services
  • Industry Knowledge: Strong understanding of IP PBX, VoIP, SIP, PSTN, cloud communications, and cloud contact center technologies.
  • Technical Acumen: Ability to work closely with engineering teams, understanding APIs, integrations, and cloud and PBX-based telecom architectures.
  • Agility & Leadership: Proven experience in working with complex, scalable systems with Agile/Scrum methodologies and leading cross-functional teams.
  • Customer-Centric Mindset: Experience in gathering customer insights and translating them into impactful product features.
  • Analytical Skills: Strong ability to interpret data, track key metrics, and drive data-driven decisions.
  • Excellent Communication: Ability to clearly articulate product vision, strategy, and technical requirements to diverse audiences.


Technical


  • Experience with SIP trunking, FreeSWITCH/Asterisk, or carrier integrations
  • Knowledge of call routing systems (skill-based routing, queues)
  • Exposure to AI voice or conversational AI platforms
  • Familiarity with compliance standards for Telephony
  • Experience with AI-powered contact center solutions, omnichannel communication, workforce optimization (WFO), or workforce management (WFM) tools


Why join us?

You will own a mission-critical product line that combines Back-office collaboration (Office Phones) and IP-PBX modernization with Cloud Contact Center, Skill-based routing, and AI-driven voice automation


Your Goal

  • Eliminate fragmented telephony stacks
  • Unify front-office (agents) and back-office ( staff) communication
  • Deliver a single platform for all voice interactions


You Will Operate At The Intersection Of


  • Telephony Infrastructure (SIP, PBX, PSTN)
  • Cloud Communications Platforms
  • Real-time routing systems
  • Enterprise SaaS product design
  • AI Voice automation


About Eltropy (www.eltropy.com)

Eltropy is on a mission to disrupt the way people access financial services. Eltropy enables financial institutions to digitally engage in a secure and compliant way. Using our world-class digital communications platform, community financial institutions can improve operations, engagement and productivity. CFIs (Community Banks and Credit Unions) use Eltropy to communicate with consumers via Text, Video, Secure Chat, co-browsing, screen sharing and chatbot technology — all integrated in a single platform bolstered by AI, skill-based routing and other contact center capabilities.


Eltropy Values

  • Customers are our North Star
  • No Fear - Tell the truth
  • Team of Owners


Eltropy is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

descriptionHtml:
At Dario, Every Day is a New Opportunity to Make a Difference.

We are on a mission to make better health easy. Every day our employees contribute to this mission and help hundreds of thousands of people around the globe improve their health. How cool is that? We are looking for passionate, smart, and collaborative people who have a desire to do something meaningful and impactful in their career.


We are looking for a strong Senior Product Manager who combines strategic thinking, healthcare market expertise, data-driven decision-making, and hands-on execution leadership.

This role owns both product direction and measurable business impact — including engagement, retention, and revenue contribution.


Only candidates currently residing in Gurugram, or those willing to relocate, will be considered


What You Will Do

Strategic Product Leadership

  • Define and drive product strategy aligned with company growth objectives.
  • Identify opportunities to increase member engagement, retention, and platform utilization.
  • Analyze healthcare and digital health market trends to uncover growth and differentiation opportunities.
  • Build and maintain a clear, outcome-driven roadmap that balances innovation, scalability, and revenue impact.

Engagement & Revenue Ownership

  • Define and track KPIs tied to engagement, activation, retention, clinical outcomes, and revenue contribution.
  • Drive initiatives that improve member stickiness and long-term value.
  • Partner with business stakeholders to ensure product decisions support monetization models and expansion opportunities.
  • Continuously optimize product performance based on measurable ROI.

Data-Driven Decision Making

  • Use analytics, experimentation, and market insights to validate hypotheses and prioritize investments.
  • Lead structured discovery processes grounded in quantitative and qualitative data.
  • Make informed trade-offs based on business impact and long-term value creation.

Engineering Partnership & Execution

  • Lead planning, prioritization, and execution within a dedicated scrum team.
  • Own backlog strategy, sprint goals, and release planning.
  • Work closely with engineering, data science, and design to deliver AI/ML and GenAI-powered capabilities.
  • Balance speed, technical scalability, and business value in execution decisions.
  • Ensure solutions align with long-term platform and shared services strategy.

AI & Platform Innovation

  • Drive responsible integration of AI and Generative AI into scalable healthcare experiences.
  • Translate advanced AI capabilities (personalization, predictive insights, conversational AI) into features that increase engagement and outcomes.
  • Contribute to reusable platform capabilities across conditions and markets.

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Align engineering, clinical, compliance, and business stakeholders around clear priorities and measurable outcomes.
  • Communicate strategy, roadmap, and impact to senior leadership.
  • Ensure compliance with regulatory frameworks (HIPAA, FDA, data privacy).


Qualifications


  • 8–10 years of product management experience, ideally in digital health, healthcare SaaS, or regulated industries.
  • Experience building and scaling mobile applications (iOS and/or Android), with strong understanding of mobile product metrics, user experience, and release cycles.
  • Proven track record of driving measurable improvements in engagement, retention, and revenue.
  • Strong experience shaping product strategy based on market analysis and performance data.
  • Solid understanding of healthcare ecosystem dynamics and digital health trends.
  • Demonstrated ability to lead scrum teams and own delivery end-to-end.
  • Experience delivering AI/ML-driven capabilities in collaboration with engineering and data science.
  • Strong analytical mindset with ownership of KPIs and business outcomes.
  • Ability to confidently engage with business stakeholders while working closely with engineering.
  • Excellent English communication skills (verbal and written).
  • Self-starter who thrives in fast-paced, evolving environments.


***DarioHealth promotes diversity of thought, culture and background, which connects the entire Dario team. We believe that every member on our team enriches our diversity by exposing us to a broad range of ways to understand and engage with the world, identify challenges, and to discover, design and deliver solutions. We are passionate about building and sustaining an inclusive and equitable working and learning environments for all people, and do not discriminate against any employee or job candidate. ***

descriptionHtml:
Organizations everywhere struggle under the crushing costs and complexities of “solutions” that promise to simplify their lives. To create a better experience for their customers and employees. To help them grow. Software is a choice that can make or break a business. Create better or worse experiences. Propel or throttle growth. Business software has become a blocker instead of ways to get work done.



There’s another option. Freshworks With a fresh vision for how the world works.



At Freshworks, we build uncomplicated service software that delivers exceptional customer and employee experiences. Our enterprise-grade solutions are powerful, yet easy to use, and quick to deliver results. Our people-first approach to AI eliminates friction, making employees more effective and organizations more productive. Over 72,000 companies, including Bridgestone, New Balance, Nucor, S&P Global, and Sony Music, trust Freshworks customer experience (CX) and employee experience (EX) software to fuel customer loyalty and service efficiency. And, over 4,500 Freshworks employees make this possible, all around the world.



Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.



Job Description

We are looking for a Senior Manager – Product Analytics (Individual Contributor) to serve as the hands-on Product Analytics Lead for one of our Product Verticals, owning product analytics end-to-end and shaping product strategy through data. This is a senior IC role for an analytics leader who thrives in ambiguity, partners deeply with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Design, and influences product roadmap decisions with strong analytical rigor. You will work with complex product telemetry, customer behavior, and business data to generate actionable insights that improve customer value and business outcomes, in close collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams. You will also mentor 2–4 product analysts, acting as a subject-matter expert and raising the bar for analytics quality, experimentation, and storytelling across the organization.

The Impact You Will Create

  • Shape Product Strategy: You will own end-to-end product analytics for a dedicated vertical, transforming complex product telemetry and customer behavior data into actionable insights that directly dictate the product roadmap.

  • Define the North Star: You will establish, evolve, and own the core metrics and KPIs that align Product, Engineering, Marketing, and GTM teams around customer value and business outcomes.

  • Champion an Experimentation Culture: You will lead rigorous A/B testing initiatives from hypothesis to executive interpretation, embedding a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across all product teams.

  • Elevate the Analytics Standard: Acting as a subject-matter expert, you will mentor 2-4 product analysts and raise the organizational bar for data integrity, advanced statistical analysis, and compelling executive storytelling.

Responsibilities

  • Own end-to-end product analytics for a Product Vertical with high autonomy and ownership
  • Partner closely with Product Managers, Engineering, and Design leaders to influence product strategy and roadmap decisions
  • Collaborate with Marketing, Sales, Customer Success, and GTM teams to support growth, adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives through data
  • Translate ambiguous business and product questions across teams into structured analytical problem statements
  • Define, evolve, and own north-star metrics and product KPIs aligned to customer and business outcomes
  • Act as the product analytics owner for instrumentation and telemetry, driving event design, coverage, and data quality
  • Work with Engineering, TPMs, and Data Engineering to ensure scalable, reliable, and trusted data foundations
  • Lead A/B testing and experimentation initiatives from hypothesis to analysis and interpretation
  • Apply statistical techniques (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) to inform decisions
  • Perform deep-dive analyses on large, complex datasets to uncover root causes and high-impact opportunities
  • Build and maintain analytics products such as dashboards, frameworks, and self-serve insights for product and business stakeholders
  • Communicate insights through clear, compelling narratives and executive-ready presentations to senior leadership
  • Mentor 2–4 junior product analysts, providing guidance on analysis design, experimentation, and storytelling
  • Champion best practices in analytics, including data integrity, validation, documentation, and reproducibility
  • Foster a data-driven, experimentation-led culture across product teams

Qualifications

Qualifications & Skills

Core Experience

  • 10+ years of total experience in analytics or data science, with 5–7+ years of hands-on product analytics experience.

  • Deep domain expertise in B2B SaaS environments is highly preferred.

Technical & Data Stack

  • Data Querying & Scripting: Expert proficiency in SQL for managing large, complex datasets, paired with strong hands-on Python skills for exploratory, statistical, and predictive analysis.

  • Data Visualization: Proven experience building and maintaining self-serve analytics products and dashboards using Power BI (or similar tools).

  • AI/GenAI Leverage: Exposure to utilizing AI and GenAI tools to enhance and accelerate analytics workflows.

Analytical Methodologies & Product Thinking

  • Advanced Experimentation: Deep understanding of statistical methods (regression, cohort analysis, confidence intervals, causal analysis) and A/B testing design.

  • SaaS Product Metrics: Expertise in product telemetry, event instrumentation design, funnel analysis, and cohort-based lifecycle analysis.

  • Product Sense: Strong business orientation with the ability to connect raw data insights directly to customer adoption, retention, and expansion initiatives.

Leadership & Communication

  • Executive Storytelling: Excellent communication skills with a proven track record of translating ambiguous data into clear narratives and executive-ready presentations.

  • Strategic Influence: Proven ability to operate independently in ambiguous environments and influence senior product, engineering, and business leadership.

  • Mentorship: Experience guiding and mentoring junior analysts in analysis design, data validation, and storytelling best practices.



Additional Information

At Freshworks, we have fostered an environment that enables everyone to find their true potential, purpose, and passion, welcoming colleagues of all backgrounds, genders, sexual orientations, religions, and ethnicities. We are committed to providing equal opportunity and believe that diversity in the workplace creates a more vibrant, richer environment that boosts the goals of our employees, communities, and business. Fresh vision. Real impact. Come build it with us.

title: Senior Product Manager (Integrations)
description: Senior Product Manager (Integrations) |100% Remote | EltropyWe are seeking a detail-oriented and driven Product Manager – Integrations to join our fast-growing global team. In this role, you will own the strategy and execution of Eltropy’s integrations platform, enabling partners and financial institutions to seamlessly connect with our ecosystem. You will play a critical role in building and scaling our developer platform and partner integration capabilities, enabling integrations with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, account opening solutions, CRMs, eSignature/eNotary providers, and digital banking platforms. This role will also help drive Eltropy’s AI for All vision, powering an ecosystem where partners can build intelligent solutions and AI agents through our growing agentic AI marketplace.This is a remote position based in India, with 10% travel to client sites in the U.S. as needed.What You OfferMaster’s degree (or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a closely related field.Minimum of 3 years of experience in Product Management, Software Engineering, or a technical product role.Experience working with APIs, integrations, or platform-based products.Proficiency in Agile/Scrum workflows and cross-functional collaboration.Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical needs into clear product requirements.Experience owning end-to-end product lifecycles, from ideation and roadmap planning to release and adoption.Technical Skills (Required)Experience using Atlassian’s Confluence and JIRA suite of toolsExperience working with REST APIs and integration architecturesExperience using Agile development methodologiesExperience using data analysis and reporting toolsBasic understanding of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JSON, APIs)Key Assets (Highly Valued)Experience working with financial institutions, fintech platforms, or financial services softwareFamiliarity with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, digital banking platforms, or CRMsExperience building or managing developer platforms or API ecosystemsUnderstanding of how community banks and credit unions operateExperience supporting partner integrations or marketplace ecosystemsExposure to AI-powered platforms, automation tools, or agent-based AI systems
descriptionHtml:
Senior Product Manager (Integrations) |100% Remote | Eltropy

We are seeking a detail-oriented and driven Product Manager – Integrations to join our fast-growing global team. In this role, you will own the strategy and execution of Eltropy’s integrations platform, enabling partners and financial institutions to seamlessly connect with our ecosystem. You will play a critical role in building and scaling our developer platform and partner integration capabilities, enabling integrations with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, account opening solutions, CRMs, eSignature/eNotary providers, and digital banking platforms. This role will also help drive Eltropy’s AI for All vision, powering an ecosystem where partners can build intelligent solutions and AI agents through our growing agentic AI marketplace.

This is a remote position based in India, with 10% travel to client sites in the U.S. as needed.

What You Offer

  • Master’s degree (or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, or a closely related field.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in Product Management, Software Engineering, or a technical product role.
  • Experience working with APIs, integrations, or platform-based products.
  • Proficiency in Agile/Scrum workflows and cross-functional collaboration.
  • Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to translate complex technical needs into clear product requirements.
  • Experience owning end-to-end product lifecycles, from ideation and roadmap planning to release and adoption.

Technical Skills (Required)

  • Experience using Atlassian’s Confluence and JIRA suite of tools
  • Experience working with REST APIs and integration architectures
  • Experience using Agile development methodologies
  • Experience using data analysis and reporting tools
  • Basic understanding of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JSON, APIs)

Key Assets (Highly Valued)

  • Experience working with financial institutions, fintech platforms, or financial services software
  • Familiarity with core banking systems, loan origination platforms, digital banking platforms, or CRMs
  • Experience building or managing developer platforms or API ecosystems
  • Understanding of how community banks and credit unions operate
  • Experience supporting partner integrations or marketplace ecosystems
  • Exposure to AI-powered platforms, automation tools, or agent-based AI systems

descriptionHtml:
Job Requisition ID #

26WD96615

Position Overview

Autodesk is a global leader in design and make technology. From architecture, engineering, and construction to media and entertainment, to manufacturing, Autodesk empowers innovators everywhere to solve the big challenges. Our solutions give design and make teams the power to connect their data, workflows, and teams so they can achieve the unimaginable. Are you ready to shape the future of making?

Autodesk is seeking a passionate and experienced Senior Product Manager to spearhead the development and implementation of our Voice of the Customer (VoC) program. This pivotal role will be responsible for establishing a comprehensive strategy for capturing, analysing, and disseminating customer feedback across the organization. A key focus will be on partnering closely with our Customer Success organization to integrate VoC insights into workflows, use in companywide decision making, ensuring a closed-loop feedback system that drives continuous improvement in our products, services, and overall customer experience.

As the Product Manager for VoC & Customer Success Integration, you will be a champion for our customers, ensuring their voices are heard and acted upon. You will define the vision, strategy, and roadmap for our VoC program, working collaboratively with cross-functional teams including Product, Engineering, Customer Success, Support, Marketing, and Analytics. Your ability to translate customer insights into actionable strategies and drive their implementation will be critical to our success.

Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Vision: Define and articulate the vision, strategy, and roadmap for Autodesk's Voice of the Customer program & systems, aligning it with overall business outcomes and customer experience goals
  • Program Development & Implementation: Lead the end-to-end development and implementation of the VoC program, including identifying key customer touchpoints, selecting appropriate data collection methodologies (surveys, interviews, social listening, etc.), and establishing processes for data management and analysis
  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner closely with Customer Success leadership and teams to understand their needs and identify opportunities to integrate VoC insights into their workflows, including onboarding, adoption, support, and renewal processes
  • Feedback Loop Design: Design and implement effective feedback loop mechanisms within Customer Success workflows to ensure customer feedback is systematically captured, analyzed, and acted upon
  • Insights Generation & Communication: Develop and implement processes for analyzing VoC data to identify key trends, pain points, and opportunities for improvement. Communicate these insights effectively to relevant stakeholders across the organization through compelling reports, presentations, and dashboards
  • Prioritization & Action Planning: Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to prioritize customer-driven improvements and translate feedback into actionable product requirements and development initiatives
  • Tooling & Technology: Evaluate and recommend appropriate VoC platforms, analytics tools, feedback acquisition, and integrations to support the program's objectives and ensure efficient data collection and analysis
  • Performance Monitoring & Optimization: Define key metrics to measure the effectiveness of the VoC program and its impact on customer satisfaction, retention, and advocacy. Continuously monitor performance and identify opportunities for optimization and improvement
  • Advocacy & Education: Champion the importance of the Voice of the Customer across the organization, fostering a customer-centric culture and educating teams on how to leverage VoC insights in their work
  • Staying Current: Keep abreast of industry best practices and emerging trends in VoC methodologies, technologies, and customer experience management

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Computer Science, or a related field. MBA or advanced degree is a plus
  • 10+ years of experience in product management, customer experience, market research, or a related field, with a strong focus on Voice of the Customer programs
  • Proven track record of successfully designing, implementing, and managing VoC programs in a SaaS or technology environment
  • Deep understanding of various VoC methodologies and tools, including survey design, qualitative research, social listening, and analytics platforms
  • Experience working closely with Customer Success organizations and integrating customer feedback into their workflows
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights and present findings effectively
  • Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence and collaborate effectively across all levels of the organization
  • Demonstrated ability to drive cross-functional initiatives and manage complex projects
  • Passion for understanding customer needs and advocating for their perspective
  • Experience with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) and Gainsight is a plus
  • Familiarity with Autodesk products and the design and make industries is a plus

Learn More

About Autodesk

Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made.

We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world.

When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us!

Salary transparency

Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience and geographic location. In addition to base salaries, our compensation package may include annual cash bonuses, commissions for sales roles, stock grants, and a comprehensive benefits package.

Diversity & Belonging

We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity-and-belonging

Are you an existing contractor or consultant with Autodesk?

Please search for open jobs and apply internally (not on this external site).

title: Senior AI Product Manager
description: We are looking for an experienced AI Product Manager who can operate at the intersection of product, technology, client engagement, and execution. This is a high-ownership, enterprise-facing role for someone who is excited by the opportunity to shape cutting-edge AI applications, work directly with international clients, and translate complex business problems into scalable AI-enabled products.The AI Product Manager will lead the definition, shaping, and execution of enterprise AI products and features for global clients. This role requires a rare mix of product judgment, AI fluency, consulting maturity, and hands-on problem solving. You will work closely with clients, engineering teams, designers, and delivery leaders to identify opportunities, define product direction, manage expectations, and bring AI-first solutions to life.The ideal candidate understands how to apply GenAI and agentic patterns in enterprise contexts, is comfortable navigating ambiguity, and is hands-on enough to prototype ideas, validate concepts quickly, and sharpen requirements through action. This is not a purely roadmap or coordination role. We are looking for someone who can think strategically, engage credibly with clients, and get into the detail when needed.Key ResponsibilitiesOwn the product definition and execution of enterprise AI applications, workflows, and features from discovery through release and adoptionWork directly with clients and internal stakeholders to identify business problems, frame opportunities, and convert within client accountsContribute to a product and delivery culture that values speed, clarity, experimentation, accountability, and customer impactWhat Success Looks Like In This RoleEnterprise AI constraintsProduct requirements are crisp, actionable, and reduce ambiguity for delivery teamsAI use cases move beyond demos into usable workflows that solve real operational problemsTeams make faster, better decisions because product direction, scope, and trade-offs are well managedClient confidence improves through strong communication, attention to detail, and disciplined expectation managementYou help create momentum for account expansion by spotting practical product and AI opportunities that clients want to invest inRequirements7-14 years of experience in ProApplication formDesign the application form for this role.duct Management, Business Analysis, Consulting, Enterprise Software, Digital Products, or related technology rolesDemonstrated experience working on software products, platforms, or enterprise applications in client-facing environmentsGood understanding of AI/ML and Generative AI concepts, with practical exposure to AI-enabled product design and deliveryExposure to building or shaping agent-based / agentic workflows or AI-driven assistants/copilots in a product or solution contextStrong experience in requirements discovery, user story definition, prioritization, stakeholder management, and cross-functional executionAbility to engage confidently with enterprise clients, business users, engineering teams, and senior internal stakeholdersStrong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complexity and present with clarityHigh attention to detail and the ability to manage product quality and client expectations in parallelA hands-on mindset with the willingness to prototype, experiment, and validate ideas quickly rather than rely only on documentationComfort working in ambiguous, fast-evolving environments where AI capabilities, client needs, and product direction are still being shapedPreferred QualificationsPrior experience in a consulting or client advisory environment is highly desirableExposure to enterprise domains and business applications is preferredExperience supporting farming-led account expansion, identifying opportunities within existing accounts, or helping grow strategic client relationships is a strong plusBackground as a Software Engineer or strong technical foundation is preferredFamiliarity with enterprise workflow systems, platform integrations, business process automation, or complex multi-stakeholder applications is a plusExperience creating basic prototypes independently using AI coding tools, prompt-based development, wireframing, or lightweight design toolsStrong educational background from top-tier institutions is preferred, including IIMs, IITs, BIT Mesra, BITS Pilani, top NITs, or equivalent institutionsSkills And Capability AreasAI product thinking and practical GenAI applicationAgentic workflow understanding and AI feature conceptualizationEnterprise product management and requirements rigorClient-facing communication and consulting maturityHands-on prototyping, experimentation, and validationCross-functional collaboration with engineering, UX, and deliveryStrong execution discipline and attention to detailCommercial awareness and account growth mindsetWho Will Thrive HereYou are likely to thrive in this role if you are someone who enjoys building in fast-moving environments, likes solving messy enterprise problems, and can move comfortably between strategy and execution. You are structured but not rigid, hands-on but not narrow, and client-focused without losing product depth. You bring ownership, curiosity, and pragmatism. You know how to earn trust in a room, ask the right questions, and turn ambiguity into actionYou should also enjoy working at the frontier of enterprise AI, where the best solutions require not only product thinking and technical awareness, but also judgment, experimentation, and strong partnership with clients and delivery teams Why Join AccellorThis is an opportunity to play a meaningful role in shaping real enterprise AI products, not just internal experiments or slideware. At Accellor, you will work on cutting-edge AI applications for global clients, solve complex business problems, and build products that matter in real operating environments.You will gain exposure to international clients, cross-functional teams, and high-impact business contexts. You will have the opportunity to influence both product direction and delivery outcomes, while building deep experience in one of the most important technology shifts underway todayFor the right candidate, this role offers a strong platform for growth across AI product management, enterprise transformation, consulting, and global client leadershipLocation / Working ModelThis role is based in Hyderabad or Bangalore. Travel may be required based on client and project needs.Reporting StructureThis role reports to the Head of Global Delivery and will work closely with leadership across Product, Engineering, Design, Delivery, and Client Engagement.BenefitsExciting Projects: We focus on industries like High-Tech, communication, media, healthcare, retail and telecom. Our customer list is full of fantastic global brands and leaders who love what we build for them.Collaborative Environment: You Can expand your skills by collaborating with a diverse team of highly talented people in an open, laidback environment — or even abroad in one of our global centers.Work-Life Balance: Accellor prioritizes work-life balance, which is why we offer flexible work schedules, opportunities to work from home, and paid time off and holidays.Professional Development: Our dedicated Learning & Development team regularly organizes Communication skills training, Stress Management program, professional certifications, and technical and soft skill trainings.Excellent Benefits: We provide our employees with competitive salaries, family medical insurance, Personal Accident Insurance, Periodic health awareness program, extended maternity leave, annual performance bonuses, and referral bonus
descriptionHtml:
We are looking for an experienced AI Product Manager who can operate at the intersection of product, technology, client engagement, and execution. This is a high-ownership, enterprise-facing role for someone who is excited by the opportunity to shape cutting-edge AI applications, work directly with international clients, and translate complex business problems into scalable AI-enabled products.

The AI Product Manager will lead the definition, shaping, and execution of enterprise AI products and features for global clients. This role requires a rare mix of product judgment, AI fluency, consulting maturity, and hands-on problem solving. You will work closely with clients, engineering teams, designers, and delivery leaders to identify opportunities, define product direction, manage expectations, and bring AI-first solutions to life.

The ideal candidate understands how to apply GenAI and agentic patterns in enterprise contexts, is comfortable navigating ambiguity, and is hands-on enough to prototype ideas, validate concepts quickly, and sharpen requirements through action. This is not a purely roadmap or coordination role. We are looking for someone who can think strategically, engage credibly with clients, and get into the detail when needed.

Key Responsibilities

  • Own the product definition and execution of enterprise AI applications, workflows, and features from discovery through release and adoption
  • Work directly with clients and internal stakeholders to identify business problems, frame opportunities, and convert them into clear product requirements and delivery plans
  • Shape AI-first user experiences, including copilots, assistants, agentic workflows, decision-support tools, and human-in-the-loop enterprise applications
  • Translate ambiguous business needs into structured product artifacts such as problem statements, user journeys, PRDs, acceptance criteria, prioritization frameworks, and release scope
  • Partner closely with engineering, UX, architecture, and delivery teams to ensure product intent is translated into practical, scalable solutions
  • Drive workshops, requirement discovery sessions, stakeholder reviews, demos, and alignment discussions with enterprise clients and senior stakeholders
  • Build lightweight prototypes, proof-of-concepts, and product mock flows using modern AI tools, no-code/low-code methods, vibe coding, and basic design/prototyping techniques where useful
  • Help define and refine AI use cases involving agents, orchestration, retrieval, workflow automation, and enterprise system integration
  • Maintain a sharp eye for detail across business requirements, product behavior, user experience, and delivery quality
  • Manage client expectations proactively by balancing ambition, technical feasibility, timelines, and delivery realities
  • Support value realization and account growth by identifying follow-on product opportunities, expansion areas, and innovation pathways within client accounts
  • Contribute to a product and delivery culture that values speed, clarity, experimentation, accountability, and customer impact

What Success Looks Like In This Role

  • Enterprise AI products and features are clearly defined, well-prioritized, and shipped with strong adoption potential
  • Clients see you as a credible product partner who understands both business value and execution constraints
  • Product requirements are crisp, actionable, and reduce ambiguity for delivery teams
  • AI use cases move beyond demos into usable workflows that solve real operational problems
  • Teams make faster, better decisions because product direction, scope, and trade-offs are well managed
  • Client confidence improves through strong communication, attention to detail, and disciplined expectation management
  • You help create momentum for account expansion by spotting practical product and AI opportunities that clients want to invest in

Requirements

  • 7-14 years of experience in ProApplication formDesign the application form for this role.duct Management, Business Analysis, Consulting, Enterprise Software, Digital Products, or related technology roles
  • Demonstrated experience working on software products, platforms, or enterprise applications in client-facing environments
  • Good understanding of AI/ML and Generative AI concepts, with practical exposure to AI-enabled product design and delivery
  • Exposure to building or shaping agent-based / agentic workflows or AI-driven assistants/copilots in a product or solution context
  • Strong experience in requirements discovery, user story definition, prioritization, stakeholder management, and cross-functional execution
  • Ability to engage confidently with enterprise clients, business users, engineering teams, and senior internal stakeholders
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complexity and present with clarity
  • High attention to detail and the ability to manage product quality and client expectations in parallel
  • A hands-on mindset with the willingness to prototype, experiment, and validate ideas quickly rather than rely only on documentation
  • Comfort working in ambiguous, fast-evolving environments where AI capabilities, client needs, and product direction are still being shaped

Preferred Qualifications

  • Prior experience in a consulting or client advisory environment is highly desirable
  • Exposure to enterprise domains and business applications is preferred
  • Experience supporting farming-led account expansion, identifying opportunities within existing accounts, or helping grow strategic client relationships is a strong plus
  • Background as a Software Engineer or strong technical foundation is preferred
  • Familiarity with enterprise workflow systems, platform integrations, business process automation, or complex multi-stakeholder applications is a plus
  • Experience creating basic prototypes independently using AI coding tools, prompt-based development, wireframing, or lightweight design tools
  • Strong educational background from top-tier institutions is preferred, including IIMs, IITs, BIT Mesra, BITS Pilani, top NITs, or equivalent institutions

Skills And Capability Areas

  • AI product thinking and practical GenAI application
  • Agentic workflow understanding and AI feature conceptualization
  • Enterprise product management and requirements rigor
  • Client-facing communication and consulting maturity
  • Hands-on prototyping, experimentation, and validation
  • Cross-functional collaboration with engineering, UX, and delivery
  • Strong execution discipline and attention to detail
  • Commercial awareness and account growth mindset

Who Will Thrive Here

  • You are likely to thrive in this role if you are someone who enjoys building in fast-moving environments, likes solving messy enterprise problems, and can move comfortably between strategy and execution. You are structured but not rigid, hands-on but not narrow, and client-focused without losing product depth. You bring ownership, curiosity, and pragmatism. You know how to earn trust in a room, ask the right questions, and turn ambiguity into action
  • You should also enjoy working at the frontier of enterprise AI, where the best solutions require not only product thinking and technical awareness, but also judgment, experimentation, and strong partnership with clients and delivery teams

Why Join Accellor

This is an opportunity to play a meaningful role in shaping real enterprise AI products, not just internal experiments or slideware. At Accellor, you will work on cutting-edge AI applications for global clients, solve complex business problems, and build products that matter in real operating environments.

  • You will gain exposure to international clients, cross-functional teams, and high-impact business contexts. You will have the opportunity to influence both product direction and delivery outcomes, while building deep experience in one of the most important technology shifts underway today
  • For the right candidate, this role offers a strong platform for growth across AI product management, enterprise transformation, consulting, and global client leadership

Location / Working Model

This role is based in Hyderabad or Bangalore. Travel may be required based on client and project needs.

Reporting Structure

This role reports to the Head of Global Delivery and will work closely with leadership across Product, Engineering, Design, Delivery, and Client Engagement.

Benefits

Exciting Projects: We focus on industries like High-Tech, communication, media, healthcare, retail and telecom. Our customer list is full of fantastic global brands and leaders who love what we build for them.

Collaborative Environment: You Can expand your skills by collaborating with a diverse team of highly talented people in an open, laidback environment — or even abroad in one of our global centers.

Work-Life Balance: Accellor prioritizes work-life balance, which is why we offer flexible work schedules, opportunities to work from home, and paid time off and holidays.

Professional Development: Our dedicated Learning & Development team regularly organizes Communication skills training, Stress Management program, professional certifications, and technical and soft skill trainings.

Excellent Benefits: We provide our employees with competitive salaries, family medical insurance, Personal Accident Insurance, Periodic health awareness program, extended maternity leave, annual performance bonuses, and referral bonus

descriptionHtml:
About Learneo
Learneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal.

Role Overview
Now we’re building a full AI-powered creation platform that supports them from the first spark of an idea to a finished, shareable piece of work.

The creation suite is a key pillar of this evolution, the space where users can transform their words into visually compelling assets: presentations, campaigns, PDFs, social posts, and more.

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to lead this effort and shape the future of how people create with QuillBot.

Responsibilities

  • Lead the evolution of the creation suite from MVP to a flagship QuillBot product—driving the roadmap, success metrics, and user experience.
  • Own the end-to-end user journey for visual creation—from ideation to layout to export.
  • Partner with engineers, designers, marketing, brand, SEO and researchers to turn text and ideas into adaptive, multimodal outputs.
  • Define and validate workflows, templates, and design systems that make non-designers feel like creators.
  • Collaborate closely with the Writing and Chat product teams to ensure seamless handoffs between idea, draft, and design.
  • Use data, research, and product intuition to balance user delight, technical feasibility, and business impact.
  • Lead a cross-functional pod toward measurable outcomes—traffic, engagement, retention, and satisfaction.

Qualification 

  • 6+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years building/growing creation or design-centric products.
  • A track record of taking consumer-facing tools from 0→1 or 1→N, ideally with products in the creative or productivity space.
  • Experience in AI-assisted or automation-heavy workflows—text-to-image, auto-layout, or generative creation—is highly desirable.
  • Deep understanding of UX for creative tools and empathy for non-technical users.
  • Strong design sensibility—you understand layout, visual hierarchy, and what makes creation feel effortless.
  • Data-driven mindset; comfort working with analytics to inform decisions and measure success.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills across design, engineering, marketing, and leadership.

What You Can Expect to Do in Your 180 Days

First 30 Days: Learn & Align

  • Deep-dive into QuillBot’s creation platform vision, user personas, and current MVP.
  • Understand the existing creation suite experience, performance metrics, and user pain points.
  • Conduct a competitive benchmarking.
  • Build strong partnerships with Design, Engineering, and AI/UX Research leads.
  • Align on near-term success metrics and team priorities.

30-90 Days: Define & Prioritize

  • Craft a clear roadmap for post-MVP development—defining which workflows, templates, and features matter most.
  • Lead early experiments in user experience, template expansion, and AI-assisted creation.
  • Establish analytics and feedback loops for measuring product success.
  • Partner with marketing and growth to position the creation suite within QuillBot’s ecosystem.

90–180 Days—Execute & Scale

  • Launch new workflows or features that significantly improve user creation speed and output quality.
  • Define and validate the long-term strategy for multimodal, brand-aware creation.
  • Drive engagement growth and user retention through data-backed iteration.
  • Build the foundation for QuillBot’s primary visual creation surface.

Why You’ll Love Working Here

  • Own one of the most impactful growth and experimentation surfaces at QuillBot.
  • Shape how millions of users discover and first experience QuillBot.
  • Work at the intersection of AI, growth, and product systems.
  • Collaborate with a team that values speed, learning, and thoughtful product decisions.
  • Be a key driver of QuillBot’s transition from individual tools to a cohesive creation platform.

Benefits & Perks

  • Competitive salary and annual bonus
  • Medical coverage
  • Life and accidental insurance
  • Vacation & leaves of absence (menstrual, flexible, special, and more!)
  • Developmental opportunities through education & developmental reimbursements & professional workshops
  • Maternity & parental leave
  • Hybrid & remote model with flexible working hours
  • On-site & remote company events throughout the year
  • Tech & WFH stipends & new hire allowances
  • Employee referral program
  • Premium access to QuillBot

*Benefits and benefit amounts differ by region. A comprehensive list applicable to your region will be provided in your interview process.

Research shows that candidates from underrepresented backgrounds often don't apply for roles if they don't meet all the criteria. We strongly encourage you to apply if you're interested: we'd love to learn how you can amplify our team with your unique experience!

This role is eligible for hire in India.

We are a virtual-first company and have employees dispersed throughout the United States, Canada, India, Israel, Germany, and the Netherlands. We have a market-based pay structure that varies by location. The base pay for this position is dependent on multiple factors, including candidate experience and expertise, and may vary from the amounts listed. You may also be eligible to participate in our bonus program and may be offered benefits and other types of compensation.

#Learneo

Equal ​Employment Opportunity Statement ​(EEO)

We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity and ​inclusion within our company.  ​We will consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender ​identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, ​veteran status, or ​ability status. We will ensure that individuals who are differently abled are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment as provided to other applicants or employees. Please contact us to request accommodation.

descriptionHtml:
Overview

Senior Staff Product Manager – Virtual Expert Platform

Join Intuit’s Virtual Expert Platform (VEP) team as a Senior Staff Product Manager and shape the future of platform innovation at scale. This is a high-impact role where you will drive the strategy, design, and delivery of platform-critical capabilities that connect millions of consumers, small businesses, and accountants to the right experts across all Intuit product lines.

We are seeking an exceptional platform product manager who brings deep experience in building and scaling mission-critical platforms. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with strong technical acumen, proven ability to drive cross-company adoption, and a track record of delivering enterprise-grade platforms that unlock customer and business value.

Responsibilities

What You’ll Do

  • Own and define the platform vision and strategy: Set a bold, multi-year direction for capabilities in Virtual Expert Platform, ensuring it meets the needs of diverse customers and scales across Intuit’s ecosystem.
  • Drive platform architecture and scale: Partner closely with engineering and architecture to design resilient, extensible, and scalable platform solutions that power core customer and expert experiences.
  • Enable platform adoption and reusability: Deliver services and APIs that are broadly adopted across multiple product lines, accelerating innovation and ensuring consistency in customer experiences.
  • Leverage AI and data at scale: Harness AI/ML and data-driven insights to deliver personalized, proactive, and intelligent experiences across the platform.
  • Execute with impact: Translate vision into strategy and roadmaps, drive prioritization, and deliver measurable outcomes aligned with customer success and company growth.
  • Build organizational alignment: Collaborate with senior executives, cross-functional teams, and business partners to ensure platform initiatives have adoption, visibility, and strategic alignment.
  • Continuously optimize: Improve platform operating models, processes, and governance to maximize scalability, quality, and speed.

Qualifications

What We’re Looking For

  • Proven platform expertise: 8+ years of product management experience with significant time spent on platform products—driving adoption across multiple product lines or business units.
  • Track record of scaling platforms: Demonstrated success in designing and scaling mission-critical, high-availability platforms that power core business functions.
  • Strong technical foundation: Ability to engage deeply with engineering on architecture, APIs, scalability, and integration. Experience with AI/ML-driven products is a strong plus.
  • Customer-first mindset: Passion for understanding customer needs, with a history of translating insights into platform strategies that deliver real value.
  • Business and execution leadership: Data-driven decision-making, excellent prioritization, and a record of delivering impactful results in complex environments.
  • Influential leadership: Strong ability to collaborate, influence, and align executives, engineering, and cross-functional teams around a shared platform vision.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field required; MBA or equivalent experience preferred.

Intuit provides a competitive compensation package with a strong pay for performance rewards approach. This position may be eligible for a cash bonus, equity rewards and benefits, in accordance with our applicable plans and programs (see more about our compensation and benefits at Intuit®: Careers | Benefits). Pay offered is based on factors such as job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and work location. To drive ongoing fair pay for employees, Intuit conducts regular comparisons across categories of ethnicity and gender. The expected base pay range for this position is:

Thousands of businesses across Southeast Asia use Aspire cards every day and we’re scaling rapidly to new geographies, currencies, features and customer segments. descriptionHtml:
About this Role:


Cards are the core growth engine for Aspire - driving spend, customer engagement across our platform. Thousands of businesses across Southeast Asia use Aspire cards every day and we’re scaling rapidly to new geographies, currencies, features and customer segments. This requires deep thinking our card products are built - from issuing infrastructure, partner integrations, internal operations, and card propositions.

As Senior Product Manager, Cards, you will own the strategy, roadmap and execution of Aspire’s card programs end-to-end. This means shaping our multi-geography and multi-currency product vision; leading integrations with card networks, issuer processors, banking partners; and building internal infrastructure that lets us scale reliably. You’ll work hand-in-hand with engineering, design, operations, compliance and commercial teams to ship products that our customers and partners genuinely rely on.

This is a high ownership role at the intersection of product, infrastructure and business.


What you’ll do?

  • Own the cards roadmap: Define the product strategy and roadmap for card programs; be accountable for launch and growth performance
  • Build and scale issuing infrastructure: Lead integrations with networks, issuers, and processors; ensure reliable authorization, clearing, settlement, and reconciliation at scale
  • Own project delivery: Lead complex, cross-functional integrations / initiatives, working collaboratively with product, engineering, operations, compliance and commercial; own delivery timelines and quality
  • Own partner relationships: Manage and negotiate with networks, issuers, processors and payment partners
  • Build for compliance: Ensure all programs meets the regulatory, scheme and security standards of the markets we operate in
  • Be deeply technical and data-driven: Go deep on technical mechanics of card program ; comfortable on technical with engineering


What we are looking for?

Core experience:

  • 5+ years as a Product Manager, ideally in cards, fintech, or banking infrastructure
  • Track record of owning and scaling a product, not just shipping features


Operator mindset:

  • Sharp problem-solving and attention to detail
  • Bias to action
  • Ability to drive complex, cross-functional initiatives across multiple stakeholders


Technical depth:

  • Strong understanding of system design, SDLC, flows, APIs
  • Able to work closely with Product and Engineering and make informed technical decisions
  • Solid understanding of AI concepts and experience applying AI tools to monitoring, anomaly detection, risk signals, or operational automation.


Communication:

  • Clear, structured communicator with the ability to influence internal and external stakeholders.


Preferred qualifications:

  • Hands-on experience launching or scaling card programs (0>1; 1>10) Exposure to multi-geo card programs
  • Background in engineering, data or fintech

title: Lead Product Manager - Star Trek Fleet Command
description: Scopely is looking for a Lead/ Principal Product Manager to join our Star Trek Fleet Command team in Bangalore, India on a hybrid basis.At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day—whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers developing, publishing, and innovating in the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily.Star Trek™ Fleet Command is a top-grossing MMO 4X strategy game that has captivated players for over five years. As we continue to expand and evolve the game, we are seeking a highly driven and analytical Senior Product Manager to own the performance and strategic direction of our live content, including monthly events, offers, and player experience.What You’ll DoAs Lead Product Manager, you’ll be the key driver for identifying, executing, and optimizing strategies that improve player engagement, retention, and monetization across our live game. You will focus on key LiveOps pillars such as events, feature performance, and live sales; ensuring that everything we launch is performant, data-informed, and player-first.You’ll work cross-functionally with Product Marketing, Product Ops, and Analytics teams to define, prioritize, and deliver exceptional player experiences that meet both business and player goals.Key Responsibilities IncludeThis position involves taking ownership of LiveOps excellence and driving the success of our LiveOps operations through effective leadership, critical thinking and flawless execution.Drive the performance strategy for LiveOps systems, recurring events, and monetization features, ensuring they meet player expectations and business KPIs.Perform analysis and optimization of live features and events, using data to identify opportunities, perform root-cause diagnostics, and execute impactful improvements.Contribute to building a repeatable, scalable approach to LiveOps performance management and experimentation.Partner with UX, Research, and Community teams to develop empathy for player sentiment and pain points, and translate them into actionable product improvements.Develop clear documentation and KPIs for live operations, continuously iterating on performance with a test-and-learn mindset.Mentor junior PMs and contribute to a culture of product excellence and cross-functional collaboration.What We’re Looking For5+ years of experience in product management, ideally with significant live operations responsibility in a F2P mobile game.Demonstrated success managing complex monetization and engagement systems, with strong experience in event design, sales strategy, or player lifecycle management.Data-native mindset, with the ability to translate raw performance metrics into strategic insights and actionable plans.Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a proven track record of aligning diverse teams around a shared product vision.Strong player empathy and experience working with user research, community feedback, or other voice-of-player channels.Experience driving impact across cross-functional pods, including creative, design, and engineering teams.Work ScheduleThis role follows a non-standard working schedule to support close collaboration with a U.S.-based team. The position requires a regular daily overlap from 9:30 PM to 11:30 PM IST to align with the Los Angeles team (this overlap is a fixed requirement). During the initial ramp-up period, new hires are also expected to be available for collaboration during daytime hours, approximately between 10:30 AM and 4:30 PM IST, with some flexibility. Candidates should be comfortable working a split schedule during the ramp-up phase, with at least two hours of guaranteed overlap with the U.S. team.About ScopelyScopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.
descriptionHtml:
Scopely is looking for a Lead/ Principal Product Manager to join our Star Trek Fleet Command team in Bangalore, India on a hybrid basis.

At Scopely, we care deeply about what we do and want to inspire play every day—whether in our work environments alongside our talented colleagues or through our deep connections with our communities of players. We are a global team of game lovers developing, publishing, and innovating in the mobile games industry, connecting millions of people around the world daily.

Star Trek™ Fleet Command is a top-grossing MMO 4X strategy game that has captivated players for over five years. As we continue to expand and evolve the game, we are seeking a highly driven and analytical Senior Product Manager to own the performance and strategic direction of our live content, including monthly events, offers, and player experience.

What You’ll Do

As Lead Product Manager, you’ll be the key driver for identifying, executing, and optimizing strategies that improve player engagement, retention, and monetization across our live game. You will focus on key LiveOps pillars such as events, feature performance, and live sales; ensuring that everything we launch is performant, data-informed, and player-first.

You’ll work cross-functionally with Product Marketing, Product Ops, and Analytics teams to define, prioritize, and deliver exceptional player experiences that meet both business and player goals.

Key Responsibilities Include

  • This position involves taking ownership of LiveOps excellence and driving the success of our LiveOps operations through effective leadership, critical thinking and flawless execution.
  • Drive the performance strategy for LiveOps systems, recurring events, and monetization features, ensuring they meet player expectations and business KPIs.
  • Perform analysis and optimization of live features and events, using data to identify opportunities, perform root-cause diagnostics, and execute impactful improvements.
  • Contribute to building a repeatable, scalable approach to LiveOps performance management and experimentation.
  • Partner with UX, Research, and Community teams to develop empathy for player sentiment and pain points, and translate them into actionable product improvements.
  • Develop clear documentation and KPIs for live operations, continuously iterating on performance with a test-and-learn mindset.
  • Mentor junior PMs and contribute to a culture of product excellence and cross-functional collaboration.

What We’re Looking For

  • 5+ years of experience in product management, ideally with significant live operations responsibility in a F2P mobile game.
  • Demonstrated success managing complex monetization and engagement systems, with strong experience in event design, sales strategy, or player lifecycle management.
  • Data-native mindset, with the ability to translate raw performance metrics into strategic insights and actionable plans.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a proven track record of aligning diverse teams around a shared product vision.
  • Strong player empathy and experience working with user research, community feedback, or other voice-of-player channels.
  • Experience driving impact across cross-functional pods, including creative, design, and engineering teams.

Work Schedule

This role follows a non-standard working schedule to support close collaboration with a U.S.-based team. The position requires a regular daily overlap from 9:30 PM to 11:30 PM IST to align with the Los Angeles team (this overlap is a fixed requirement). During the initial ramp-up period, new hires are also expected to be available for collaboration during daytime hours, approximately between 10:30 AM and 4:30 PM IST, with some flexibility. Candidates should be comfortable working a split schedule during the ramp-up phase, with at least two hours of guaranteed overlap with the U.S. team.

About Scopely

Scopely is a leading video game and global interactive entertainment company, home to many of the world’s most beloved and enduring experiences, including two of the most successful mobile games of all-time “MONOPOLY GO!” and “Pokémon GO,” along with “Stumble Guys,” “Star Trek™ Fleet Command,” “MARVEL Strike Force,” “WWE Champions,” the Scrabble® franchise, “Yahtzee® With Buddies,” and many others. Across mobile, web, PC, and console, Scopely creates, develops, publishes, and live-operates one of the most diversified and award-winning portfolios in the games industry — bringing hundreds of millions of players together through a shared love of play.

Founded in 2011, Scopely is powered by its exceptional team — including thousands of world-class gamemakers around the globe, a distinctive tenet-driven culture, and its proprietary technology platform, Playgami. Together, these strengths have fueled Scopely’s position as the #1 mobile games company in the U.S. and #2 globally, generating more than $10 billion in lifetime revenue. Whether building global sensations like “MONOPOLY GO!” from the ground up, or expanding through strategic acquisitions, including the FoxNext, GSN, and Niantic games businesses — Scopely consistently delivers experiences players love today and return to for years to come.

Recognized multiple times as one of the "100 Most Influential Companies in the World" by TIME magazine and one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies" and “Best Workplaces for Innovators,” Scopely believes that video games can be a force for good — creating meaningful connections, vibrant communities, and making life better through play.

Scopely has global operations and partners across four continents in more than a dozen countries worldwide. For more information, visit: https://www.scopely.com/.

Notice to Candidates: Scopely will never request payment or financial information during the application or hiring process. Please apply only through our official website and verify that all Talent Partner communications come from an email address ending in @scopely.com.

Should you have any questions or encounter any fraudulent requests/emails/websites, please immediately contact recruiting@scopely.com. Our job applicant privacy policies are available here: California Privacy Notice and EEA/UK Privacy Notice.

Employment at Scopely is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications. Scopely does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.
